name: PROPS
description: First microbial opsin-based voltage indicator, though only functional in bacteria and extremely dim.

spectrum.name: PROPS
spectrum.note: Real excitation + emission digitized 2026-06-13 from Kralj 2011 Fig. 1A (purified PROPS in solution). Excitation = the pH 9.0 curve (dark purple): pH 9.0 is below the Schiff-base pKa of 9.6, so the retinal SB is protonated — the pink, NIR-fluorescent state — giving the dominant alpha-band excitation peak at ~555 nm with a low blue/B-band (400-490 nm); the higher-pH deprotonated forms (pH 10.2/10.9) instead show a large ~420 nm band and a deep ~490 nm trough, and were NOT used. Emission = the black curve, broad NIR peak (published peakEm 710 nm; the digitized curve peaks ~699 nm within the broad ~695-712 nm plateau). Each curve normalized to its own max = 1.0 at 1 nm. minEm starts at 623 nm (the emission's blue-edge valley) because the rising blue tail overlaps the declining excitation in the ~600-625 nm crossover. peakEx 555 / peakEm 710 kept as the published label values.

voltage.name: PROPS
voltage.note: Digitized by pixel-tracing the red voltage data in Fig. 1C, a dual-axis raw-Fluorescence (A.U. 1-7) plot vs membrane voltage (bottom red axis, red data) and pH (top blue axis, blue curve). RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-04 against a prior-sweep QA flag by an independent axis calibration keyed to the numeral-label centers (not the frame lines): x-numerals at px 270.5/366.5/472.5/572.0/674.0 for -0.2/-0.1/0/+0.1/+0.2 V give a clean linear fit px = 1012.5*V + 471.1 (0 V @ px 471, 101.25 px per 0.1 V); y-numerals 1-7 centered at py 386.5..78.3 give 51.37 px per unit (F=1 @ py 386.5, NOT the frame bottom at py 411). The red curve reads F(-150)=1.43, F(-130)=1.58, F(-110)=1.89, F(-90)=2.34, F(-70)=2.68, F(-50)=3.19, F(-30)=3.80, F(-10)=4.16, F(+10)=4.70, F(+30)=5.12, F(+50)=5.35, F(+70)=5.69 A.U., converted to ΔF/F normalized to F(-70)=2.68 -> 0. Physical anchor confirms the calibration: the paper states fluorescence is 'five times as bright at +70 mV than at -170 mV' (main text) - here F(+70)/F(~-170)=5.69/~1.14=5.0x. (A QA reviewer's alternative calibration placed 0 V @ px 452.5 / 90.75 px per 0.1 V and F=1 @ py 411, which implied F(-170)=0.49 - physically impossible and a ~11.6x ratio - so that calibration was rejected; the numeral-center fit above is definitive.) The headline ΔF/F=150% per 100 mV (Fig. 1C caption) is this same -70->+30 step normalized to the dark-state minimum fluorescence (Fmin~1.1-1.4 A.U.): ΔF over -70->+30 = 5.12-2.68 = 2.44 A.U., /Fmin ~= 175-220%; normalized to F(-70) the same step is +91% - the F-V curve below uses the site-wide -70 mV = 0 convention.

kinetics.note: Single unresolved time constant of 4.7 ms for the fluorescence response to positive and negative steps in induced transmembrane voltage (Fig. 1D, average of 20 voltage pulses); on and off were not separately reported, so identical on/off is the paper's value, not a placeholder. Room-temperature recording (Methods: 'All experiments were performed at room temperature'). NOTE: 4.7 ms is the voltage-step response constant, NOT the cell-wide blink FWHM (blinks last 1-40 s).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F = 150% per 100 mV, stated explicitly in the Fig. 1C caption ('The sensitivity was ΔF/F = 150% per 100 mV'). Measured by widefield epifluorescence (633 nm HeNe excitation, 1P) via induced transmembrane voltage (ITV) in intact E. coli. This IS the -70->+30 mV (100 mV) step (correct window, not a full-range artifact), but normalized to the dark-state minimum fluorescence (Fmin~1.1-1.4 A.U.) rather than F(-70): from the re-verified Fig. 1C raw-fluorescence curve (2026-07-04), ΔF over -70->+30 = 5.12-2.68 = 2.44 A.U., /Fmin = ~175-220%. The same step normalized to F(-70)=2.68 is +91% (the convention used by the stored F-V curve, voltage.note). Main text also notes fluorescence was 'five times as bright' at +70 mV than at -170 mV.
